title: A10. Riemann Hypothesis Super-Rigidity Gap
label: gemma426b_clean_benchmark_v1_2026_04_06
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: atlas-derived-A10-riemann-hypothesis-super-rigidity-gap
Title: A10. Riemann Hypothesis Super-Rigidity Gap

Question:
Can a Hermitian matrix eigenvalue spectrum match the Riemann zeta zeros' 47% super-rigidity (Delta_3_sat = 0.155)?

Grading target:
Binary:

- correct conclusion
- identification of the quantitative gap: 0.155 vs 0.24

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt: FINAL_ANSWER: No JUSTIFICATION: The Riemann zeta zeros exhibit a super-rigidity characterized by a Delta 3 statistic of approximately 0.155, whereas the eigenvalues of a standard Hermitian matrix (GUE) follow a different distribution with a Delta 3 value of approximately 0.24, representing a quantitative gap.
final_result_excerpt: FINAL_ANSWER: No JUSTIFICATION: The Riemann zeta zeros exhibit a super-rigidity characterized by a Delta 3 statistic of approximately 0.155, whereas the eigenvalues of a standard Hermitian matrix (GUE) follow a different distribution with a Delta 3 value of approximately 0.24, representing a quantitative gap.

root_orchestration.decision_notes: The question asks for a binary comparison of spectral statistics.

grading.expected:
No.

Best construction achieves Delta_3_sat = 0.24.
GUE statistics are easy, but super-rigidity requires prime-orbit structure no matrix encodes.
grading.extracted_answer: No JUSTIFICATION: The Riemann zeta zeros exhibit a super-rigidity characterized by a Delta 3 statistic of approximately 0.155, whereas the eigenvalues of a standard Hermitian matrix (GUE) follow a different distribution with a Delta 3 value of approximately 0.24, representing a quantitative gap.
